name: Derbyshire (United Kingdom)
description: A light-blue field with a green cross fimbriated white, charged at the centre with a gold (yellow) Tudor rose.
history: Adopted in 2006 following a poll in the Derby Telegraph and registered with the Flag Institute. The cross uses the green of the Peak District and the colours of Derbyshire's traditional rose; the Tudor rose recalls the dual White-and-Red rose tradition.

features.symbolism: blue for the Derbyshire skies, green for the Peak District landscape
